meta.issuer_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司

company.name_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司
company.headquarters: Building 1, No. 88 Dongliu Road, Xixing Street, Binjiang District, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China
company.industry: C3499 Other General Equipment Manufacturing (not elsewhere classified)

products.name: B Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: High-performance industrial-grade quadruped robot; applications include scientific research, industrial inspection, emergency rescue, and intelligent services

products.name: Go Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: Consumer-grade quadruped robot; Go1 achieved running speed of 4.7 m/s setting a world record for similar-spec quadruped robots; cumulative sales exceeding 30,000 units during the reporting period

products.name: H1 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: First self-developed general-purpose humanoid robot released August 2023; achieved world's first high-difficulty electric-drive backflip; achieved running speed exceeding 5 m/s setting a full-size humanoid robot world record; applications include scientific research, application development, education, cultural performance, and intelligent services

products.name: G1 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: Mid-size general-purpose humanoid robot released 2024; achieved electric-drive side flip in March 2025; 100m obstacle race completed in 33.71 seconds at World Robot Games 2025; featured in CCTV Spring Festival Gala AI-driven group performance

products.name: H2 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: Next-generation full-size humanoid robot; participated in autonomous swarm martial arts performance 'Wu BOT' alongside 24 G1 units in early 2026

products.name: Joint Module
products.specs: High-performance self-developed joint module; core actuator component for humanoid and quadruped robots

products.name: Dexterous Hand
products.specs: High-degree-of-freedom robotic end-effector simulating human hand functions; multi-joint, multi-modal sensing with intelligent control for complex object grasping and manipulation

products.name: Collaborative Robotic Arm
products.specs: Self-developed collaborative robotic arm component for general-purpose robot ecosystem

products.name: Perception Sensors (LiDAR and other sensors)
products.specs: Self-developed high-performance sensors including LiDAR, depth cameras, millimeter-wave radar, and other perception devices for environment sensing

products.name: Embodied Intelligence Model (Embodied Body Intelligence Model + Embodied Large Model)
products.specs: Full-stack self-developed AI models: Embodied Body Intelligence Model (Physical Intelligence, robot 'cerebellum') enabling high-stability, high-complexity whole-body agile motion; Embodied Large Model (Cognitive Intelligence, robot 'brain') enabling environment understanding, intent perception, autonomous decision-making and task execution; includes WMA (World-Model-Action) and VLA (Vision-Language-Action) large model technologies

products.name: High-Performance Electric Motor
products.specs: Self-developed high-performance electric motor reaching industry-leading level; core execution unit installed at robot joints to control joint movement

products.name: Reducer (Planetary Gearbox)
products.specs: Self-developed precision reducer using planetary gear transmission; high precision, high rigidity, high efficiency; core transmission device for robot joints

shareholders_pre_ipo.name: Robot Fund (Beijing Robot Industry Development Investment Fund)
Wuxi Jinqiu (Wuxi Jinqiu Hua Lei Technology)

key_risks: Risk of technology breakthroughs and product innovation falling short of expectations: The high-performance general-purpose robot industry involves frequent technological innovation with high upfront investment and significant uncertainty. Embodied intelligence technology (comprising physical intelligence models and embodied large models) is still in R&D and testing stages globally. If the company or the industry fails to achieve important technological advances in embodied large models, or if the company makes poor decisions in R&D and product development, or key technologies cannot be breakthroughed and performance targets are not met, it will adversely affect the company's competitive advantages and operating results.

Risk of downstream large-scale commercial applications falling short of expectations: Large-scale commercialization of humanoid robots still faces uncertainty. Current technical maturity in areas such as generalization capability of embodied large models and dexterity/durability of robotic hands is limited. The consumer-level market for general-purpose robots has not yet formed rigid demand, the application ecosystem construction and market cultivation cycle is long, and supply chain capabilities in energy systems and real-time computing platforms have shortcomings. Regulatory policies, industry standards, and social acceptance may also constrain commercialization progress.

Risk of international trade frictions and escalation of regulatory policies: International trade frictions have created obstacles for Chinese companies conducting overseas business. Since 2025, U.S. government import tariff policies have fluctuated. Overseas revenue accounts for over 35% of the company's total revenue each period. If the U.S. continues to implement significantly unfavorable trade and tariff policies against Chinese companies, or lists the company on restricted procurement or technology export control lists, the company may be unable to maintain high growth in overseas sales or may experience a decline in performance. Additionally, imported materials purchased through domestic agents account for approximately 20% of total raw material procurement, which may also be adversely affected.

Risk of intensified industry competition and disorderly improper competition: Global tech giants are accelerating their deployment and startups are proliferating, intensifying market competition. Companies such as Tesla, with large-scale production capabilities and AI technology resources, may rapidly reduce unit costs of humanoid robots like Optimus, directly intensifying price competition and putting pressure on the company's product pricing, market share, and profit margins. The core of industry competition is shifting from hardware manufacturing to full-stack 'perception-decision-execution' technology. If the company fails to continuously increase R&D investment or fails to effectively leverage its first-mover advantages, it will face the risk of its core technological advantages being weakened.

Corporate governance risk from the special voting rights mechanism: Before this offering, actual controller Wang Xingxing controls 34.7630% of shares and, through the special voting rights arrangement, controls 68.7816% of voting rights. After this offering, Wang Xingxing's controlled voting rights ratio will drop to no more than 65.3090%. Under special circumstances, the actual controller's interests may not be consistent with other shareholders, potentially harming minority shareholders' interests.

Risk of high revenue growth rate and declining gross margin levels: Revenue growth rates from 2023 to 2025 were 29.95%, 145.83%, and 335.31% respectively, and gross margins were 44.22%, 56.41%, and 60.27% respectively. If industry competition intensifies, technology upgrades or product iterations occur, commercial application expansion is slower than expected, or the company cannot maintain technological leadership, the company may face declining gross margins and weakened profitability.

Risk of key technology leakage and loss of core personnel: The company has developed core technologies and built a core R&D team over many years. If key technologies are leaked or core R&D team members leave, it will adversely affect the company's technological innovation capability, market competitiveness, and operating results.

Risk of rapid growth in inventory and inventory write-down losses: At the end of each reporting period, the company's inventory book value was RMB 77.5374 million, RMB 78.9577 million, RMB 140.5805 million, and RMB 278.0366 million respectively, with relatively high proportions of current assets. If raw material prices, supply chain, or market conditions change adversely, the net realizable value of relevant products may significantly decrease, increasing inventory write-down risks.

Risk of rapid growth in accounts receivable and bad debt losses: Accounts receivable balances at the end of each reporting period were RMB 7.8467 million, RMB 11.0671 million, RMB 21.5948 million, and RMB 83.4565 million respectively. If downstream customers' financial conditions deteriorate, there is a risk that accounts receivable cannot be recovered.

Risk of large new investment and expenditure from fundraising projects: The fundraising investment projects will generate significant depreciation, amortization, and expense expenditures during implementation. If the industry development trend or market environment changes significantly for the worse, causing the projects to fail to achieve expected returns, the new depreciation, amortization, and period expenses will adversely affect future operating results.

Foreign exchange fluctuation risk from overseas revenue settled in foreign currency: Overseas main business revenue accounted for 57.21%, 55.63%, 55.70%, and 39.20% of total main business revenue respectively during the reporting periods. The company's overseas sales are primarily settled in USD. Intensified USD/RMB exchange rate fluctuations or significant changes in exchange rate policies will affect the company's performance.

Risk of operating performance decline due to multiple factors: If new technology development or new product promotion falls short of expectations, raw material costs rise, R&D investment intensity increases, large share-based payment expenses from newly granted equity incentives occur, and the company fails to take effective countermeasures in time, the company will face the risk of a significant decline in operating performance or even losses.

Risk of social attention hotspots and market demand fluctuations: If progress in key technology breakthroughs, cost control, or scenario applications is slow, current market enthusiasm and capital attention may significantly decrease. Long-term commercialization of humanoid robots requires overcoming technical reliability, production costs, market acceptance, and legal/regulatory challenges. Periodic demand fluctuations may directly affect the industry's and company's capacity utilization and revenue realization.

Risk of stock-based compensation impacting financial results: The company recognized share-based payment expenses of RMB 18.7226 million and RMB 349.0655 million in 2022 and 2025 respectively from capital increases to Shanghai Yuyi equity incentive platform. Future equity incentive grants will continue to generate share-based payment expenses that may significantly impact the company's future period operating results.

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Model Research and Development Project
projects.focus: Continuous R&D investment in embodied intelligence models including physical intelligence models (cerebellum) and embodied large models (brain); building cloud-edge-end computing infrastructure; constructing large-scale real datasets and automated annotation mechanisms; building intelligent development and training platforms integrating data engines, model factories, and evaluation centers to support model training, deep learning, and application expansion.

projects.name: Robot Body (Hardware) Research and Development Project
projects.focus: R&D of high power density motors, high power density transmission systems, high power density drivers, multi-scale lightweight structural components for legged robots, and centralized thermal control systems; consolidating the company's body performance advantages and cost advantages in high-performance general-purpose robot products.

projects.name: New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project
projects.focus: Accelerating the iteration of various types of robot products (humanoid robots, quadruped robots, and extended products such as dexterous hands, dexterous robotic arms, 4D LiDAR); enriching the product matrix; strengthening key R&D links including overall architecture design, core component adaptation, functional performance testing and optimization, and process design; increasing applicable scenarios and strengthening market competition barriers.

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project
projects.focus: Purchasing and constructing a production base to solve the problems of limited site area and insufficient production capacity; introducing automated assembly lines in the production area; configuring precision testing instruments in the inspection area for full-process testing of product performance, motion accuracy, and safety; deploying intelligent warehousing systems with IoT and automated equipment; realizing data integration across production, inventory, and procurement to improve per capita output efficiency and reduce unit production costs.
